cards.back: A method of regulating the access to a shared resource, ensuring that only authorized users or devices can access the network at any given time.
Examples include CSMA/CD, token ring, and MAC address filtering.

cards.front: How does CSMA/CD controlled access work?
cards.back: It uses a random backoff mechanism to resolve collisions, ensuring only one device can access the network at a time.

cards.front: What is the main advantage of MAC address filtering?
cards.back: It provides a high level of security by only allowing devices with specific MAC addresses to access the network.

cards.front: How does token ring controlled access work?
cards.back: It uses a central token that is passed from device to device, allowing each device to access the network only when the token is theirs.
